content: I am a bit confused. In the 'server settings' pane for an account, you can specify whether to get mail at startup. And there is an icon on the main screen to get all messages. So, I do not understand the problem. Do those settings not work? Thank you. Maybe we just need more information.
creator: davidsk

content:
Thanks david,

In the Thunderbird "server settings" for "Get mail at startup", I had some checked and others not checked. So, per your email, I unchecked all, and now to get the mail, all I have to do is click the extension "Get All Email." That's what I was looking for. Thanks.
creator: cannonball70

content:
I answered this recently, showing two options. Here is that link:

https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/questions/1569866

The first option requires a spreadsheet and has worked quite well for many users. The second option also works, but requires careful selection of the desired fiels. But it does work if you identify the key fields.

AND, if you have a thunderbird backup, you can look for a file named abook.sqlite. that can be imported with tools>import>import from a file.
creator: davidsk

content: The first option seems to be a lot of manual labor, so I took a look at the second. That one does not work because the drop down list is only populated with one item and that is the one that is already selected which is the wrong one. So I cannot correct it to the right field.
